description_long: This bungalow features a long, rectangular structure with a prominent black gable roof that extends the full length of the building. The exterior walls are light gray, punctuated by a series of evenly spaced rectangular windows along both long sides, providing ample natural light. The front facade includes a small covered porch with blue accents and visible support columns, adding a welcoming touch. The rear facade is simpler, with a central door and minimal ornamentation. The building sits low to the ground, typical of bungalow architecture, and the roof overhangs slightly to provide shade and protection. The overall design is functional and modest, suitable for residential neighborhoods.
description: A long, single-story bungalow with a black gable roof, light gray walls, and a small front porch with blue accents.
description_view_0: Front view showing the small covered porch with blue accents and the main entrance.
description_view_1: Side view displaying a row of evenly spaced windows along the length of the building.
description_view_2: Rear view with a central door and minimal decorative features.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, mirroring the window arrangement of the other side and showing a secondary entrance.
